learning_outcomes.description: Have an understanding of the key psychological foundations of visitor experiences.
Explain how visitor demographics, psychographics and motives influence decision making and behaviour of visitor experiences.
Through the usage of an infographic, redesign an existing visitor experience.

assessments.due_date:
28/08/2026 2:00 pm

Both vlog and the one-page document are due on the same date, same time.
assessments.task_description:
Visitor experiences can provide great value; however, they can sometimes be outdated or irrelevant and may fail to attract targeted visitors. New competitors or substitute products may also become available. Therefore, plans need to be continually refreshed to ensure that the visitor experience is competitive and relevant to target markets.  

Select an experience provider (real) and conduct an appraisal of an existing experience. You will incorporate demographics, geographics, psychographics, and decision-making from a visitor’s perspective to appraise the experience. This must be a recent experience you have had, and you will need photos/videos you took (preferably with you in them) at the site/venue. 

Visitor Experiences sites can include:  

  • A theme park, zoo, aquarium, botanical gardens or cultural attraction  
  • An interpretation centre, museum, art gallery, heritage/historic site  
  • A winery, brewery, and working farm  
  • A festival or event  
  • Other (check with your Tutor) 

Part A 

You will produce a YouTube-style vlog documenting your visit to and appraisal of a real visitor experience site. The video must be filmed in landscape format, and you must appear on screen. You may record continuously at the site, compile a series of clips and/or photos taken there, or use a combination of both approaches, but all footage and images must be your own and taken during a genuine visit. 

Your video must cover all four content sections below, in order: 

  • Introduction - Briefly introduce the site: its name, location, type of experience, and its current target audience. Show the site on camera and talk the viewer through what they are seeing. 
  • Market Segment/s and Motives - Identify and profile the market segment/s you will target with your redesigned experience, applying the segmentation techniques covered in class (demographics, geographics, psychographics, decision-making). Apply one motivation theory or model from the course; do not mix and match models, to explain why your chosen segment would be motivated to engage with the redesigned experience. 
  • Need for Redesign - Explain and justify why this experience needs to be redesigned. Use SCEPTICAL factors or other relevant course frameworks. Do not simply list numerous superficial reasons why it should be redesigned. It does not need to be major or revolutionary. It might be about focusing more on a specific market, the need for a new site management procedure, or an updated promotion. Think about what is feasible and would be deemed relevant by both the experience provider and the potential visitors. 
  • Conclusion - Summarise why the redesign would benefit the experience provider and why your target segment is the right focus. Speak directly to the camera for this section. 

Video requirements: 

  • Vlog length should be 7-8 minutes.  
  • Filmed in landscape (16:9) - vertical/portrait footage will not be accepted 
  • You must appear on screen (face visible). 
  • All photos and video footage must be your own, taken during a genuine site visit. 
  • Background music is optional; if used, ensure it does not obscure your commentary. 
  • Video must be submitted to Blackboard via the EchoVideo deep linking tool. 

Part B

Alongside your video, you must submit a single-page professionally formatted summary document. The 1-pager is not a script or transcript; it is a concise, standalone reference document that communicates the essence of your appraisal. 

A template will be provided on Blackboard to complete this part.  

The 1-page document must include: 

  • Your full name, preferred name, student number, and tutorial group. 
  • Site name, location, and type of visitor experience. 
  • Your identified target market.
  • A complete APA 7 reference list. 
  • A declaration of any AI or MT tools used, with specific instances noted.

Submission 

Submit both the video file and the 1-page PDF/Word document to Blackboard by the due date.  

Further information will be provided on Blackboard and in Tutorials.
assessments.submission_guidelines: This experience appraisal vlog is to be submitted to Blackboard using the EchoVideo Deep Linking tool. The one-page document is to be submitted in PDF format to the same submission link.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
To complete this assessment task, you are required to write a response while critically reflecting on and responding to a scenario or challenge presented during your tutorials.  

This scenario or challenge will focus on creating memorable visitor experiences while linking to the weekly course content. You are required to demonstrate the professional and applied skills you have learned in class and through your readings.  

You will be assigned weekly readings, and the expectation is that you use this knowledge to inform your discussion for the tutorials before attempting the task. 

This is a pen-and-paper task to be completed in person on the workbook provided during tutorials and returned to your tutor upon completion. The use of electronic devices or notes is not permitted while completing this task.  

This assessment will take place during that week's allocated tutorial time and will be completed in class. You must attend the tutorial you are enrolled in.  

The design of the task (best 3 of 4 attempts) provides built-in flexibility. A practice session will be held in your tutorials during week 6.

Format

Pen and Paper/Handwritten and In-person during tutorials. Accessible submission format is available if you are on a Student Access Plan (SAP). Please discuss this with the teaching team.

Word limit: 400-500 words, approximately 1 page.

More details will be provided on Blackboard.
assessments.submission_guidelines: This task is required to be completed in-person during the tutorials (Pen and Paper submission). You are required to attend the tutorial you are enrolled in.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.late_submission: There are no late penalties for the in-person worksheet. It must be completed during the tutorial.
assessments.additional_info: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

assessments.task_description:
As the Experience Design Manager of your site, you will now create a redesign proposal based on the feedback and insights gathered in Assessment 1.

The proposal should focus on improving the existing experience based on the core concepts from the course, such as the Orchestra Model, Experiencescape, Visitor Management, and Interpretation. You will present a clear and professional plan to enhance visitor engagement, operational efficiency, and overall visitor satisfaction.

The proposal should be presented as a formal business document, with a focus on practical, actionable strategies. Your proposal will include an implementation plan, expected outcomes, and a timeline, allowing you to demonstrate both your analytical skills and your ability to think strategically in a business context.

The entire report (proposal) should be presented creatively, integrating infographics within the report itself. The visuals should be part of the document to enhance the reader’s understanding and engagement, not as a separate document. You are encouraged to make the proposal visually appealing while maintaining its professional and business-focused structure. (Examples will be provided during the tutorials.) 

Proposal Structure:

Executive Summary

Provide a brief summary of the proposal, outlining the current challenges, the proposed changes, and the expected outcomes of the redesign.

Introduction to the Site

In this section, provide an overview of the site you are redesigning. Include details such as the name, description, location, and current visitor experience. Highlight the target market of the site.

Current Visitor Experience and Challenges

Analyse the current visitor experience, identifying key challenges (e.g., overcrowding, lack of engagement). Include the Orchestra Model (Current) to explain how the five elements (sensory, affective, cognitive, behavioural, relationships) apply to the current experience.

Consider including this as a table. 

Proposed Redesign Strategy

Present the proposed redesign strategy, addressing improvements in:

  • Orchestra Model (Redesign): Focus on one element (sensory, affective, cognitive, behavioural, relationships) to enhance. 
  • Experiencescape: Propose physical or environmental changes to improve visitor flow and engagement. (See Bitner, 1992) servicescape- (ambient conditions, spatial layout).
  • Visitor Management: Offer strategies to optimise queues, crowding, and staff interactions.
  • Interpretation: Suggest improvements in how information is shared with visitors (e.g., digital displays, storytelling).

Implementation Plan

In this section, provide a clear timeline of key milestones for the proposed redesign. Since this is a smaller-scale redesign, the milestones should focus on immediate actions that are achievable within a short to medium timeframe.

Post-Experience Outcomes 

You should also describe how you will evaluate, monitor and improve experience quality and visitor satisfaction. What will you do to encourage repeat business or to develop an ongoing relationship with visitors? How will you help visitors create, share and recall positive memories of their experiences?

References 

A Reference List should be included in APA7 style. As a bare minimum, a pass assignment should contain at least ten (10) references from a variety of sources, including websites, books, reports, journal articles and other sources.

Further information will be provided on Blackboard and in Tutorials.

Word Count

2000 - 2500 words (+/- 10%)
assessments.submission_guidelines: This experience design project is to submitted via Turnitin.
